title: English 12 Literary and Comparative Analysis Comments
description: English 12 starter comment bank for literary and comparative analysis aligned to grades 11-12 2023 NJSLS-ELA references. These materials are teacher-editable starter review materials aligned to common high school ELA writing tasks and 2023 NJSLS-ELA references. They are not official state curriculum, not district grading policy, and not automatic evaluation.

comments.label: Interpretive Claim strength
comments.short_text: Your interpretive claim presents an interpretation instead of only a summary, which helps the reader understand the w....
comments.text: Your interpretive claim presents an interpretation instead of only a summary, which helps the reader understand the work with precision and attention to complexity.
comments.follow_up_prompt: What is one place where you can strengthen interpretive claim?
comments.revision_action: Keep this interpretive claim move and apply it in another paragraph or section.

comments.label: Interpretive Claim developing
comments.short_text: Your interpretive claim is on the right track, but it will be stronger if you make the claim more interpretive by nam....
comments.text: Your interpretive claim is on the right track, but it will be stronger if you make the claim more interpretive by naming what the text suggests.
comments.follow_up_prompt: What is one place where you can strengthen interpretive claim?
comments.revision_action: Make the claim more interpretive by naming what the text suggests.

comments.text: Your revision next steps is on the right track, but it will be stronger if you choose one high-impact revision target before editing smaller details.

comments.revision_action: Choose one high-impact revision target before editing smaller details.

comments.label: Interpretive Claim hard to follow
comments.short_text: The interpretive claim is difficult to follow right now.
comments.text: The interpretive claim is difficult to follow right now. Revisit this part first so the reader can understand the purpose of the writing.
comments.teacher_note: Use when interpretive claim is a major barrier to understanding.
comments.follow_up_prompt: Where should the student begin revising interpretive claim?
comments.revision_action: Rewrite the section that most affects interpretive claim before polishing sentences.

comments.label: Conventions next revision pass
comments.short_text: For the next revision pass, focus on conventions: Revise one moment so the explanation addresses significance, comple....
comments.text: For the next revision pass, focus on conventions: Revise one moment so the explanation addresses significance, complexity, or audience impact.
comments.follow_up_prompt: What single revision move would most improve conventions?
comments.revision_action: Make one focused pass for conventions and then reread the full piece.

comments.label: Revision Next Steps next revision pass
comments.short_text: For the next revision pass, focus on revision next steps: Revise one moment so the explanation addresses significance....

module_details.notice: These materials are teacher-editable starter review materials aligned to common high school ELA writing tasks and 2023 NJSLS-ELA references. They are not official state curriculum, not district grading policy, and not automatic evaluation.
module_details.emphasis: precise and knowledgeable claims, significance, logical sequencing of claims and counterclaims, strongest relevant evidence, avoidance of fallacies, synthesis, sophisticated style, audience awareness, complexity, ambiguity, nuance, and independent revision.
